notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-11 (Wikipedia, Al Jazeera, alforatnews.iq, baghdadtoday.news): July 14, 1958 military movement led by Abd al-Karim Qasim and Abd al-Salam Arif ended the Hashemite monarchy in Iraq (established 1921) and proclaimed the Republic of Iraq; Arif broadcast the announcement from Iraqi radio headquarters; Qasim became PM, Defense Minister, and Commander-in-Chief.

CORRECTION to backlog premise ('Official holiday'): paid-holiday status is NOT settled — Parliament passed a holidays law including July 14, but the Presidency objected and returned it to Parliament in an appendix alongside October 3; the Council of Ministers' General Secretariat has explicitly denied an official holiday existing on this date in at least one recent year. Same pattern as the already-documented victory-day-iraq (Dec 10) nuance.

Content states this honestly (commemoration is historically fixed; paid-holiday status is announced separately/disputed each year) rather than asserting it's automatically a holiday, matching the site's established 'commemoration != confirmed paid holiday' pattern.
Avoided any political-history commentary on the revolution's legacy or the monarchy's fall, per the neutral-framing rule used for sensitive Iraq/Syria/Palestine content.
Single-country event (_countryCode: iq), aliasSlugs: [] to avoid base_country_leakage.
